notes:
EX Sandstorm (ex2), released Nov 2003, 100-card set. Vintage era (ex) booster: 9 cards/pack, 36 packs/box, NO ETB. One guaranteed rare-or-better slot per pack; remaining 8 cards are commons/uncommons plus a reverse-holo filler slot (reverse is filler, not a hit, so not modeled as a separate hit slot).

Rare-slot resolution cross-confirmed by TWO independent community-research sources that agree exactly:
- Elite Fourum 'Guide to early EX Series pull rates' gives EX Sandstorm box config: 36 rares, 6 holo rares, 6 exs per 36-pack box (commons/uncommons fill the rest). 6 holo + 6 ex + 24 non-holo rare = 36 rare-slots.
- ThePriceDex EX Sandstorm pull-rates page: 'Rare Holo EX 1 in 6 packs', 'Rare Holo 1 in 6 packs', 'Rare 1 card per pack'. 1/6 = 6 per 36-pack box, matching Elite Fourum exactly. ThePriceDex cites an Elite Fourum thread as its source.

Mapping onto this set's available buckets {rare-holo, rare, uncommon, common, ex}:
- ex: 6/36 = 0.1667
- rare-holo: 6/36 = 0.1667
- rare (non-holo): 24/36 = 0.6666
Sum = 1.0.

NO gold-star and NO secret-rare buckets used: Gold Stars debuted in EX Team Rocket Returns (Nov 2004, per Bulbapedia), a year AFTER Sandstorm; this set has no secret rare. Both are also absent from the set's available rarity buckets. Reverse-holo (the 9th-card filler slot, ~1 in 6-8 packs being a reverse rare-holo) is treated as filler per the era baseline, not a hit slot.

Confidence MEDIUM (not high): figures are community-compilation research, not a large published study, but two independent sources concur. Note Elite Fourum also flags real-world variance (later print runs averaged ~0.9 ex/box rather than the nominal 6) but the nominal/base box configuration of 6 ex + 6 holo per box is the standard modeled rate and matches ThePriceDex.
